rulings.Case Title: 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 30, 2017 line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is granted as to the second cause of action for inverse condemnation. This cause of action is not ripe and may only be brought if and when proper under Kavanau v. Santa Monica Rent Control Board (1997) 16 Cal. 4th 761. The motion is denied as to the first cause of action. The Court finds that equitable tolling is applicable in this action as the facts (as alleged in the Complaint and ascertained from judicially noticeable documents) are more similar to the facts in Addison v. State of California (1978) 21 Cal.3d 313 than Magnin v. City of Glendale (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 1102. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended And Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 30, 2017 line 1. PLAINTIFF 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended And Supplemental Complaint GRANTED. Plaintiff has met the requirements for leave to file amended and supplemental complaint. Any challenge to the sufficiency of the amended/supplemental complaint shall be brought by demurrer/judgment on the pleadings. Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file its amended supplemental complaint with corrections to account for the Court's ruling on Defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BANCAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION A CALIFORNIA VS. ZORA KOLKEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 30, 2017 line 4. PLAINTIFF BANCAL MANAGEMENT CORPORATION A CALIFORNIA Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction GRANTED as follows: Defendant's dog, Dafka, may use the covered walkway, unattended to access the backyard twice a day, once in the afternoon, between 1 p.m. and 3 p.m. and once in the evening between 8 p.m. and 10 p.m. (Kolkey Declaration, 15). The dog is not to be in the walkway for more than 3 minutes during each trip to and from the backyard. The dog is not to urinate or defecate in the walkway. If the dog becomes ill Defendant to notify the Plaintiff before any "accidents" due to illness occurs. (Kolkey Declaration, 11). If any "accidents" occur due to illness of the dog and with prior warning to the landlord about dog's condition, Defendant to clean up and sanitize within 1 hour of each "accident" without using a hose. (Kolkey Declaration, 11, 17).= (501/REQ)
